item.title: Gina Raimondo on How European Industry Is Getting Crushed
item.description: The former Commerce Secretary live at CFR.

item.title: Odd Lots: Raimondo on a Global, Non-China Trading Bloc (Podcast)
item.description: Odd Lots: Raimondo on a Global, Non-China Trading Bloc (Podcast)【原文：Odd Lots: Raimondo on a Global, Non-China Trading Bloc (Podcast)】

item.title: Russia’s Key Black Sea Oil Terminal on Fire, NASA Data Show
item.description: Russia’s key Black Sea oil terminal caught fire following an overnight drone attack, according to satellite images from NASA.

item.title: New Trump Deadline Looms as Ceasefire Push Keeps Markets on Edge
item.description: US allies are reportedly pressing for a last-minute deal with Iran, as President Donald Trump extended his deadline to Tuesday for Tehran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, keeping markets on edge over whether a breakthrough can be reached.

item.title: BOJ Keeps Options Open by Citing Dual Risks in Regional Reports
item.description: The Bank of Japan avoided fueling market expectations over the likelihood of a rate hike this month by keeping its signaling from two quarterly regional economic reports highly nuanced.

item.title: Major Fertilizer Buyer India Issues Tender as War Hits Supply
item.description: India, the world’s largest urea importer, is seeking to buy about 2.5 million tons of the key crop nutrient ahead of the monsoon sowing season as the Middle East conflict disrupts domestic production, tightening availability.

item.title: Saudis Raise Asia Oil to Record Premium as War Upends Market
item.description: Saudi Arabia has raised the price of its main oil grade to Asia to a record high premium, as a widening conflict in the Persian Gulf and Iran’s near-closure of the Strait of Hormuz convulse energy markets.

item.title: Emerging Market Stocks, Currencies Gain on Iran Ceasefire Report
item.description: Emerging market assets gained in thin holiday trading, buoyed by investor hopes for a ceasefire in the Middle East conflict.

item.title: Barito Renewables Hits Two-Year Low on Shareholding Concerns
item.description: Shares of PT Barito Renewables Energy tumbled Monday to their lowest in more than two years after the Indonesian stock exchange flagged the company for having a highly concentrated shareholder base.

item.title: Japan’s Takaichi Seeks Talks With Iran’s Leader Before Ultimatum
item.description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi said she is looking to hold talks with Iran’s leader and possibly also a separate call with Donald Trump, as the clock ticks on the US president’s latest threat to bomb key Iranian infrastructure.

item.title: Iraq Tells Buyers to Collect Crude as Its Oil Can Cross Hormuz
item.description: Iraq has told Asian traders and refiners they can load its crude as vessels carrying the country’s oil are now able to transit the Strait of Hormuz thanks to an Iranian exemption, testing buyers’ confidence in the security guarantee.

item.title: Ships With Qatar LNG Attempt First Hormuz Exit Since War Started
item.description: Two tankers carrying liquefied natural gas from Qatar appear to be heading toward the Strait of Hormuz, and an exit from the Persian Gulf would mark the first export to buyers outside of the region since the war started.

item.title: Rupee Extends Rally After Biggest Surge in 12 Years on RBI Moves
item.description: The Indian rupee climbed, extending its biggest rally in 12 years after the central bank doubled down on curbing speculation against the local currency.

item.title: Hedge Funds Turn Bullish on Wheat for First Time in Four Years
item.description: Hedge funds have turned net bullish on wheat for the first time in nearly four years, betting on higher prices driven by dry weather in the US and a shortage of fertilizer and fuel arising from the war in the Middle East.

item.title: If Only the Profanity Meant an End Was in Sight
item.description: Markets have a lot to weigh up, but optimism took a bruising over Easter weekend.

item.title: The Iran War May Be Permanently Rewiring Global Energy Security
item.description: Cambridge Professor Helen Thompson warns that energy disruption triggered by the US-Israel attack on Iran is exposing deep vulnerabilities.

item.title: Rising Rupee Hedging Costs May Further Dim India’s Appeal to Global Funds
item.description: Foreign investors earn less after protecting against currency swings.

item.title: Chinese Battery-Storage Supplier Sees Shipments Doubling in 2026
item.description: Beijing HyperStrong Technology Co., one of China’s top suppliers of energy-storage systems, expects to more than double shipments this year on robust demand at home and abroad.

item.title: Indian Bank Stocks’ $95 Billion Rout May Deepen on Macro Risks
item.description: More pain awaits Indian banks stocks — the biggest component of the country’s stock market — as the central bank’s moves in the currency market and growth shock to the economy from rising energy prices dent profit outlook.

item.title: Ye Just Made $33 Million in Two Nights. Will His Comeback Last?
item.description: The music industry is tentatively embracing the artist formerly known as Kanye West

item.title: Gold Falls as Trump Threatens Escalation of US Attacks on Iran
item.description: Gold declined after US President Donald Trump stepped up threats to destroy Iran’s power plants and Tehran showed little sign of accepting American demands to end the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Oil Rises on New Deadline as Trump Raises the Stakes Over Hormuz
item.description: Oil gained as President Donald Trump imposed a fresh ultimatum on Tehran and threatened to strike power plants and other civilian infrastructure in Iran if the Strait of Hormuz is not reopened.

item.title: Debanking Is a Confusing Nightmare. It’s at Risk of Getting Worse
item.description: Trump’s regulators are reshaping rules, potentially making it harder for banks to eject clients over suspicious behavior.

item.title: Trading Open Shadowed by Trump Escalation Threats: Markets Wrap
item.description: US President Donald Trump’s warning that he may significantly escalate the war in Iran, potentially worsening an energy-price shock that’s already upending the global economic outlook, is shadowing stock, bond and oil markets before the start of the trading week.

item.title: On NASA’s Moon Mission, the Biggest Headache Has Been the Toilet
item.description: As NASA’s Artemis II astronauts make their way to the moon this weekend, their test flight has gone surprisingly well without any major issues, save for one piece of technology giving the crew some grief: the toilet.

item.title: Hormuz Tracker: Iraq-Linked Oil Tanker Exits Hormuz Via Iran
item.description: A Suezmax oil tanker hauling about 1 million barrels of Iraqi crude exited the Persian Gulf via a northerly route through Iranian waters on Sunday morning, according to vessel-tracking data compiled by Bloomberg.

item.title: Trump Risks Confidence in US Role as Guardian of Global Shipping
item.description: Of all the things Donald Trump has done to disrupt global commerce, from levying punitive tariffs to tearing up trade deals, few would be as consequential as withdrawing and leaving the rest of the world to secure the Persian Gulf.

item.title: Chinese Bonds Near Inflection Point as Inflation Outlook Shifts
item.description: Chinese bonds may be reaching a historical turning point, with yields set to climb from record low levels as deflationary pressures ease and expectations for monetary loosening recede.

item.title: Oil Tanker Carrying Iraqi Cargo Seen Transiting Strait of Hormuz
item.description: An oil tanker carrying crude from Iraq appeared to be transiting the Strait of Hormuz chokepoint, a day after Iran said its neighbor had special exemption to use the waterway.

item.title: Nvidia Partner Hon Hai’s Sales Meet Estimates on Solid AI Demand
item.description: Hon Hai Precision Industry Co. reported a 29.7% rise in quarterly sales, a sign of sustained AI demand during the first weeks of war in the Middle East.
